description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular profile and a sloped top surface. Its exterior is predominantly gray with patches of pale lichen and subtle hints of brown and green, suggesting weathering and exposure to the elements. The rock's surface is rough and uneven, with visible crevices, chips, and natural textural variations. Some faces are more vertical and shadowed, while others are flatter and lighter in color, indicating different mineral compositions or sun exposure. The rock appears solid and dense, typical of natural stone found in outdoor environments. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or landscape feature in urban parks or along sidewalks.
description: A rugged, gray rock with angular surfaces, lichen patches, and a rough, weathered texture. Its irregular shape and solid build make it suitable as a natural urban barrier or landscape element.
description_view_0: The rock appears dark and shadowed, with a prominent angular peak and visible rough texture on the left side.
description_view_1: This view shows a lighter, flatter top surface with pale lichen patches and subtle color variations.
description_view_2: The rock's sloped side is visible, with a mix of light and dark areas and a pronounced edge running diagonally.
description_view_3: A mostly shadowed side with deep crevices and a more compact, blocky appearance.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
